Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: correct S3 Vectors API method names and request structure

The actual boto3 s3vectors API uses:
  - put_vectors (not put_object) — takes vectors list with {key, data, metadata}
  - query_vectors (not query_objects) — takes queryVector, topK, returnDistance
  - delete_vectors (not delete_object) — takes keys list

S3 Vectors also requires an indexName parameter alongside vectorBucketName
(two-level hierarchy: bucket → index). Added VECTOR_INDEX_NAME env var
(defaults to "products") to both Lambda functions.

Embedding data must be passed as {"float32": [...]} not a raw list.
Response vectors are under response["vectors"] not response["Matches"].

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

remains accurate (uses actual total_realized_pnl from bot).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(tests): lazy-read Supabase env vars to fix CI test failures

Routes were reading SUPABASE_URL/KEY at module level, but tests set
env vars in beforeEach (after module import). In CI without .env.local,
the module-level constants were empty strings → 500 instead of expected
responses. Changed to lazy getter functions so env vars are read at
request time.

Fixes: following route (8 tests), traders/[handle] route (all tests)

[ManhuaTo]) into a single entry by matching on slug. Adds SeriesPath
model to track multiple source directories per series. Chapter
navigation in the reader prefers staying on the same source.

--filter is used, returning 1000+ series instead of ~32. Also strips
[Source] prefix from titles and stores it as publisher metadata.

feat(misconfiance): Dual-Stage signal (Stade 1 estimated) (#106)

* feat(misconfiance): implement Dual-Stage signal (Stade 1 estimated)

Adds automatic estimated misconfiance signal at onboarding time,
computed from voie + moyenne_generale. Stade 2 (confirmed via Reality
Check) remains the final truth and cannot be overwritten.

Phase 1: Types + service + 53 unit tests (pure functions)
Phase 2: Migration 503 with 4 RPCs (merge/replace/admin/eligible)
Phase 3: moyenne_generale added to demographics onboarding
Phase 4: Writer Stade 1 in after() + 3 Prometheus counters
Phase 5: Paginated backfill script with --dry-run

Reviews: Patricio (types, service, SQL, writer) + Sergio (auth model)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address CodeRabbit review on PR #106

SQL migration 503:
- TOCTOU race: move confirmed guard into DO UPDATE WHERE (atomic)
- Revoke replace GRANT from authenticated (prevents forging confirmed)
- Admin RPC: add source='estimated' validation
- Backfill eligible: add ORDER BY sp.profile_4d_id (stable pagination)
- Remove unused v_existing variables

Demographics route:
- Make lycee_type/classe optional in Zod schema so handleSkip()
  ({skipped: true} without lycee_type/classe) passes parse
- Enforce lycee_type/classe presence in .refine() when not skipped

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(sql): Patricio review — NULL guard, grants, dual source doc

Migration 503:
- COALESCE on WHERE guard to avoid NULL evaluation when misconfiance
  is NULL or {} (both merge and admin RPCs)
- REVOKE ALL from all roles first, then re-grant only what's needed
  (CREATE OR REPLACE keeps stale grants)
- Document dual moyenne_generale source strategy in backfill RPC
  (user_reality_checks for backfill, demographics for runtime writer)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(misconfiance): GET returns moyenne_generale, RPCs return boolean

- demographics GET: parallel fetch sweetspot_profiles for moyenne_generale
  (was write-only — reload/edit would drop the value)
- merge + admin RPCs: RETURNS boolean (true = write, false = guard blocked)
  for observability in writer + backfill script
- replace RPC: comment explaining intentional no-grant (GARDE B Stade 2)
- Writer: uses boolean to distinguish write vs guard skip in metrics
- Backfill: uses boolean to report written vs guard-skipped counts

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: harden API client and config robustness

- Add connect_timeout (10s) to HTTP client to prevent connection hangs
- Fix mutex poisoning: use unwrap_or_else(into_inner) instead of unwrap
  to recover from poisoned mutexes in ls_cache and download workers
- Fix token refresh race condition: serialize concurrent refresh attempts
  with a mutex and double-check expiry after acquiring lock
- Atomic session file writes: write to .tmp then rename to prevent
  corruption on crash
- Warn on malformed config.toml instead of silently falling back to defaults

Replaces obsolete migration 313 (referenced pfm_letters_v2, dropped in 492).
Creates v_dashboard_overview, v_dashboard_flags, v_dashboard_complete using

(partial unique, dropped in 402). Remove from 506 + migration 507 to
drop it in prod. The non-partial constraint uq_voeux_profile4d_code_formation
(migration 402) is the correct one for ON CONFLICT in bulkUpsertVoeux.

Also fixes security_barrier verification to check all 3 views.

* fix(migration-506): move verification inside transaction, EXCEPTION on failure

Verification DO block now runs before COMMIT (atomic with view creation).
RAISE WARNING upgraded to RAISE EXCEPTION so missing views or security_barrier
abort the migration instead of silently proceeding.

commit.message:
Fix 18 edge case bugs from round 12 audit, add 10 regression tests

Models:
- ModelBase: acf guard for < 2 residuals
- LinearFactorModel: p-value mapping by name (not position) for collinear terms
- LinearFactorModel: FEC fallback when X'X is singular
- GARCHModel: convergence check, avg_sigma NaN/0 guard, df >= 1 guard
- DCCGARCHModel: convergence check, near-zero market variance guard, df >= 1,
  fallback for NA beta_t

Multi-event test statistics:
- CSectTTest: sd()=0 guard for aar_t, cumsum NA guard for car/caar
- PatellZTest: cumsum NA guard, sum(csar) na.rm=TRUE
- SignTest: cumsum NA guard
- GeneralizedSignTest: sqrt(0) guard when p_hat=0/1, cumsum NA guard
- RankTest: S_rank=0 guard, cumsum NA guard
- BMPTest: sd_sar=0 guard, cumsum NA guard for sar/csar
- CalendarTimePortfolioTest: ts_sd=0 guard, cumsum NA guard
- KolariPynnonenTest: sqrt(negative) guard when r_bar>1, sd/cumsum guards

Other:
- LogReturn: guard log(0) and log(negative) → NA
- SimpleReturn: guard division by zero lagged price → NA
- print.EventStudySummary: guard round(NULL) with %||% NA_real_

880 tests passing, 0 failures.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(voeux): resequence_voeux 23505 unique violation on soft-delete (#108)

* fix(voeux): resequence_voeux 23505 unique violation on soft-delete

Root cause: single-pass CTE UPDATE in resequence_voeux does not guarantee
row processing order. PostgreSQL may assign position N to a row while
another active row still holds position N (not yet resequenced), violating
the partial unique index uq_voeux_active_profile4d_position.

Fix: two-phase update — first negate all positions (collision-free since
original positions are unique), then assign final sequential positions.
Also improves error logging in DELETE route (message + code + details).

Sentry: [API] Error soft-deleting voeu — code 23505

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Supplement Brent prices through Mar 6 with war-period reported prices

EIA and FRED APIs have a 2-4 day reporting lag. Added fallback to
verified war-period prices from CNBC/Reuters ($81.40 Mar 3, $82.76
Mar 4, $85.41 Mar 5, $87.12 Mar 6) when yfinance also unavailable.
yfinance tried first, news-reported prices used as fallback.
